title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for profiling, event-loop health, memory, load, and capacity and demands the smallest safe performance experiment and capacity envelope with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for performance experiment and capacity envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing.
Use CPU profiles, heap snapshots, allocation, garbage collection, event-loop delay, handles, and request traces to locate causal bottlenecks safely.
Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Performance Regression Recovery
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 4264 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
